zh.Harescombe, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾哈雷斯可姆

zh.Gloucestershire, England: 英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas le Rous於大約1330年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾哈雷斯可姆時，他的父親John le Rous II34歲，母親Mable Knokyn30歲。他於大約1345年與Alice Cox在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1358年12月29日在他的家鄉享年29歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.Harescombe, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾哈雷斯可姆

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas le Rous于大约1330年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾哈雷斯可姆时，他的父亲John le Rous II34岁，母亲Mable Knokyn30岁。他于大约1345年与Alice Cox在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1358年12月29日在他的家乡享年29岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Rous
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gunter, Kurt.

variants.name: Roux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Normand, Adrien, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Michel, Pierre, Rejean, Cecile, Clovis.
nickname for a red-haired man, from Old French ro(u)s ‘red’ (from Latin russeus).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas le Rous was born about 1330, in Harescombe, Gloucestershire, England, his father, John le Rous II, was 34 and his mother, Mable Knokyn, was 30. He married Alice Cox about 1345, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 29 December 1358, in his hometown, at the age of 29, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas le Rous est né environ 1330, à Harescombe, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, John le Rous II, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mable Knokyn, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Alice Cox environ 1345, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 29 décembre 1358, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas le Rous um 1330 in Harescombe, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John le Rous II 34 und seine Mutter Mable Knokyn 30. Er hat um 1345 in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Alice Cox geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 29. Dezember 1358 im Alter von 29 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas le Rous è nato il circa 1330 nel Harescombe, Gloucestershire, England, il suo padre, John le Rous II, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Mable Knokyn, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Alice Cox circa 1345, nel Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 29 dicembre 1358, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas le Rousが約1330年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアハーエスコンベで生まれた時，父John le Rous IIは34歳，母Mable Knokynは30歳であった。約1345年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでAlice Coxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1358年12月29日に故郷で29歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas le Rous(이)가 대략 1330년 영국 그루스스터실르 하레스코브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John le Rous II(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Mable Knokyn(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Alice Cox(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1358년 12월 29일 그의 고향에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas le Rous nasceu aproximadamente 1330, em Harescombe, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John le Rous II, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Mable Knokyn, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Cox aproximadamente 1345, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 29 de dezembro de 1358, em sua cidade natal, com 29 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas le Rous родился около 1330 г. в местности харескомб, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени John le Rous II было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Mable Knokyn, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Cox около 1345 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 29 декабря 1358 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 29 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas le Rous nació aproximadamente 1330, en Harescombe, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, John le Rous II, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Mable Knokyn, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Alice Cox aproximadamente 1345, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 29 de diciembre de 1358, en su pueblo, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
